questions.prompt: Are search themes in Performance Max the same as keywords?

choices.text: No — they're soft hints to PMax's ML about which queries to consider matching, not match-typed targeting. You add 5-25 short phrases per asset group; Google's NLP interprets them alongside audience signals, product feed content, and asset content to decide which queries trigger the ad. No match types, no individual bids — PMax decides where and when based on the full signal mix. Search themes complement audience signals (which are usually the bigger lift driver) and creative assets.
Yes, but with no match types.

questions.explanation: Search themes are hints, not constraints. PMax is signal-driven, not keyword-driven.

questions.prompt: Launching first PMax campaign for an e-commerce brand. Single biggest lift driver to set up?

choices.text: First-party audience signals — upload your existing customer list as a Customer Match audience and add it as the primary audience signal for asset groups. PMax uses it to identify lookalikes and weight delivery toward likely buyers. Combined with a clean Merchant Center product feed and varied creative (5+ headlines, 4+ descriptions, 4+ images in multiple ratios, 1+ video), this is the foundation. Without strong audience signals, PMax relies on weaker inputs (search themes alone, generic in-market segments) and underperforms its potential significantly.

questions.explanation: First-party customer-list audience signals drive the biggest PMax lift.
